description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a striking red and blue color scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings supported by black struts. The fuselage is streamlined and painted blue at the front, transitioning to red towards the tail, with a wooden propeller mounted at the nose. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted red with blue and white striping. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with visible seat and control details. The landing gear is fixed and appears to be a taildragger configuration, with two main wheels and a small tail wheel. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military or stunt aircraft, with roundels on the fuselage sides.
description: A vintage red and blue biplane with dual wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed taildragger landing gear. The fuselage features roundels and a streamlined, classic design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the wooden propeller, blue nose, and the stacked red wings with black struts.
description_view_1: Top-rear view highlighting the dual red wings, open cockpit, and the red tail with blue and white stripes.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the length of the fuselage, the roundel insignia, and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the propeller, blue nose, and the symmetrical arrangement of the red biplane wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings mounted one above the other, supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, single-seat configuration.
